retrospective.friction: Two rounds of test fixes were needed. First: the listener inside createDispatcher used a dynamic import() to load deserializeApplicationData; vi.mock() hoists module-level mocks but cannot intercept dynamic imports inside async functions at runtime, so the mock was bypassed and handleSpy never fired. Fix: changed to a static top-level import. Second: the LRU test fired 1100 emits without awaiting each, then used a single-round flushPromises; with 1100 pending Promise chains only the first resolved, so call counts were wrong. Fix: used emitAsync (awaits the listener promise) in loops, and reordered the dedup-check before the evicted-ids re-dispatch to avoid secondary eviction cascades.
retrospective.root_cause: Both issues stem from the async listener pattern interacting with Vitest's mock system and Promise microtask scheduling in tight loops. The dynamic-import interception gap and the multi-chain drain problem are easy to miss when the production code looks correct but the test harness has subtly different execution semantics.
